title: GlassWorm Self-Propagating Malware Infects VS Code Extensions Using Solana Blockchain Wallet as Primary C2 Channel
description: GlassWorm infected Visual Studio Code extensions across Open VSX Registry and Microsoft Extension Marketplace, using a Solana blockchain crypto wallet as primary command-and-control infrastructure with Google Calendar as a backup channel, hunting for GitHub, Git, npm, and Open VSX accounts plus crypto wallet keys, while also dropping a RAT named Zombi for complete system control.
label: AV-400

title: GlassWorm VS Code Worm Simultaneously Targets Open VSX Registry and Microsoft Extension Marketplace for Maximum Developer Reach
description: The GlassWorm supply chain worm simultaneously infected extensions across both the Open VSX Registry used by VS Code forks and the official Microsoft VS Code Extension Marketplace, maximizing developer reach by targeting extensions available across all major VS Code ecosystem platforms regardless of which IDE variant developers used.
label: AV-200

title: IndonesianFoods Campaign Floods npm Registry with Tens of Thousands of Useless Packages to Overwhelm Security Scanning
description: A coordinated malicious campaign dubbed IndonesianFoods flooded the npm registry in November 2025 with tens of thousands of useless packages, overwhelming automated security scanning infrastructure and creating a noise floor that made detection of genuinely malicious packages significantly more difficult for security teams.
label: AV-200

title: Shai-Hulud 3.0 Emerges in December 2025 as Deliberately Constrained Stealth Release Evading Signatures from Earlier Variants
description: Shai-Hulud 3.0 appeared in late December 2025 as a deliberately constrained release observed in a single npm package, with the payload showing significant refactoring aimed at evading existing detection signatures derived from earlier Shai-Hulud variants, improving cross-platform reliability, and signaling continued evolution toward more sophisticated future attacks.
label: AV-410

title: Sygnia Q4 2025 Report Confirms Supply Chain Attacks Have Become Systemic Failure Mode Across Multiple Ecosystems Simultaneously
description: Sygnia Q4 2025 threat report confirmed that supply chain attacks evolved from isolated incidents into a systemic failure mode appearing independently across npm, PyPI, VS Code marketplaces, and vendor development environments, with attackers consistently exploiting implicit trust in developer tooling and automated build pipelines without needing new vulnerabilities.
label: AV-410

title: s1ngularity Attack Targets AI LLM Client Credentials for Claude Gemini and Amazon Q to Use AI Tools as Reconnaissance Platforms
description: The s1ngularity supply chain campaign specifically targeted configuration files and authentication tokens for AI LLM clients including Claude, Gemini, and Amazon Q Developer, aiming to leverage victims own AI assistants elevated permissions and access to sensitive development environments to enumerate additional secrets and expand the attack surface.
label: AV-400

title: s1ngularity Supply Chain Campaign Harvests 2349 Credentials from 1079 Developer Systems via Compromised Nx Packages
description: The s1ngularity supply chain campaign harvested 2,349 credentials from 1,079 developer systems between August and November 2025 through compromised Nx build system packages, establishing the connection between the Nx compromise and the subsequent broader Shai-Hulud and related attack campaigns in the npm ecosystem.
label: AV-509

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 Reuses Stolen CI Tokens to Modify Legitimate Repositories Rather Than Publishing New Malware Packages
description: Shai-Hulud 2.0 evolved beyond publishing new malicious packages to instead reusing stolen CI tokens and maintainer credentials to directly modify legitimate repositories, making detection significantly harder because the malicious changes appeared as legitimate commits from trusted maintainer accounts rather than suspicious new package publications.
label: AV-400

title: Linux Foundation Census II Study Finds 70-90 Percent of Modern Application Code is Open Source Creating Massive Supply Chain Attack Surface
description: The Linux Foundation Census II study documented that open source software makes up approximately 70 to 90 percent of modern application code, establishing the foundational scale of software supply chain attack surfaces and explaining why compromising widely-used open source packages creates cascading impact across thousands of dependent applications.
label: AV-509

title: IoT Device Firmware Supply Chain Attacks Exploit Outdated Libraries in Embedded Systems with Long Product Lifecycles
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting IoT device firmware that exploit outdated open source libraries embedded in devices with multi-year lifecycles, where manufacturers rarely patch post-release vulnerabilities in bootloader components, hardware initialization modules, and RTOS kernels creating persistent security blind spots.
label: AV-400

title: Artifactory and Container Registry Credentials Emerge as High-Value Targets for Supply Chain Credential Harvesting Campaigns
description: Security researchers documented that Artifactory and container registry credentials have become high-value targets in supply chain credential harvesting campaigns, providing access to build artifacts containing embedded secrets, Docker images with baked-in credentials, and deployment configurations used across enterprise software delivery pipelines.
label: AV-509

title: DORA NIS2 and US Critical Infrastructure Regulations Require Third-Party Supply Chain Risk Management for Software Dependencies
description: Regulatory frameworks including DORA for EU financial services, NIS2 for critical infrastructure, and US software security executive orders increasingly mandate formal third-party risk management for software supply chains, driving SBOM generation, provenance verification, and dependency audit requirements across regulated industries.
label: AV-410

title: GlassWorm Uses Google Calendar as Backup C2 Channel Making Command Infrastructure Extremely Difficult to Block
description: The GlassWorm VS Code extension worm used Google Calendar as a backup command-and-control communication channel in addition to a Solana blockchain wallet, making the malware C2 infrastructure extremely difficult to block because blocking Google Calendar would prevent legitimate developer productivity tool access.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Azure Bicep and ARM Templates Alter Cloud Resource Deployment Security Policies
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Azure Bicep and ARM template files where hidden Unicode characters alter resource property values, network security group rules, and RBAC role assignments, causing Azure deployments to provision cloud infrastructure with different security configurations from what infrastructure engineers see during template review.
label: AV-300

title: Mirai Botnet Exploits Firmware Supply Chain Vulnerabilities in IoT Devices Using Hardcoded Credentials from Unchanged Defaults
description: The Mirai botnet exploited a systemic firmware supply chain vulnerability where IoT device manufacturers shipped devices with hardcoded default credentials that users never changed, demonstrating how insecure firmware supply chain practices enable massive-scale botnet recruitment affecting millions of devices across the internet.
label: AV-400

title: Q4 2025 Supply Chain Attacks Exploit Trust Debt in Developer Tooling Identities and Software Distribution Without New Vulnerabilities
description: Sygnia Q4 2025 analysis confirmed that successful supply chain attacks consistently exploited trust debt accumulated through implicit trust in developer tooling, package identities, and software distribution channels, succeeding without exploiting new technical vulnerabilities or breaching traditional network perimeters.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in AWS CDK Infrastructure Code Alter IAM Permission Definitions and S3 Bucket Policies
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in AWS Cloud Development Kit infrastructure code where hidden Unicode characters alter IAM role permission definitions and S3 bucket policy statements, causing AWS deployments to create overly permissive or incorrectly scoped cloud resources invisible during infrastructure code review.
label: AV-300

title: Security Researchers Predict 2026 Supply Chain Attacks Will Target Maven RubyGems and NuGet Ecosystems Beyond npm and PyPI
description: GitGuardian researchers predicted that 2026 supply chain attack campaigns will expand beyond npm and PyPI to target Maven, RubyGems, and NuGet ecosystems that have weaker security controls, with attacker techniques refined through 2025 campaigns being applied to compromise Java, Ruby, and .NET package ecosystems at scale.
label: AV-509

title: State-Aligned Threat Actors Shift to Targeted Developer Account Compromise and CI/CD Manipulation for Supply Chain Access
description: Silobreaker 2025 annual review documented that state-aligned threat groups and cybercriminals shifted toward highly targeted developer account compromises and CI/CD pipeline manipulation for supply chain access, abandoning broad network intrusion in favor of precisely targeted software supply chain attacks for downstream access to multiple organizations simultaneously.
label: AV-200

title: AWS and Cloudflare Service Outages Cause Major Software Supply Chain Disruptions Affecting Organizations Without Malicious Intent
description: Silobreaker 2025 review documented that significant AWS and Cloudflare outages caused major supply chain disruptions demonstrating that supply chain fragility extends beyond malicious attacks to include availability dependencies on cloud providers and CDN services used as implicit components of modern software delivery pipelines.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Pulumi Infrastructure Code Alter Cloud Resource Tags Security Groups and Encryption Settings
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Pulumi infrastructure-as-code programs where hidden Unicode characters alter cloud resource tag values, security group ingress rules, and encryption key references, causing cloud deployments to provision resources with different security properties than what engineers see when reviewing Pulumi stack code.
label: AV-300

title: Software Vendor Update Channel Compromise Enables Malicious Updates to Be Pushed to All Customers Simultaneously
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting software vendor update distribution channels, where compromising the signing infrastructure or update server allows attackers to push malicious updates to all customers simultaneously through the same trusted mechanism used for legitimate software updates.
label: AV-410

title: Global Annual Cost of Software Supply Chain Attacks Projected to Reach 138 Billion Dollars by 2031 Driven by Registry Attacks
description: Security research projects the global annual cost of software supply chain attacks will reach 138 billion dollars by 2031, driven primarily by attacks on centralized package registries like npm and PyPI that magnify impact by compromising shared components used across millions of dependent applications simultaneously.
label: AV-200

title: SBOM Software Bill of Materials Requirements Emerge as Regulatory Tool to Detect Supply Chain Attack Exposure Across Dependencies
description: Software Bill of Materials generation requirements emerging from US executive orders and EU regulations provide organizations with the dependency visibility needed to detect supply chain attack exposure, enabling rapid identification of whether specific compromised packages are present in production software deployments.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in ES Module Import Maps Redirect Browser Module Resolution to Attacker-Controlled CDN URLs
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in browser ES Module import map JSON files where hidden Unicode characters alter module specifier to URL mappings, causing browsers to load JavaScript modules from attacker-controlled CDN endpoints instead of legitimate sources while appearing to use trusted module URLs in import map review.
label: AV-300

title: GlassWorm Drops Zombi Remote Access Trojan Granting Attackers Total Control Over Developer Systems Beyond Credential Theft
description: Beyond credential theft and account hijacking, the GlassWorm VS Code extension worm dropped a Remote Access Trojan named Zombi onto infected developer systems, providing attackers with complete persistent access to compromised machines for arbitrary command execution, file exfiltration, and lateral movement into organizational infrastructure.
label: AV-410

title: Ivanti 2025 Report Finds Only One in Three Organizations Feel Prepared to Defend Against Software Supply Chain Threats
description: Ivanti 2025 security report found that only one in three organizations feel adequately prepared to defend against software supply chain threats, despite supply chain attacks emerging as a central pillar of adversary strategy with the global cost projected to reach 138 billion dollars annually by 2031.
label: AV-200

title: Dynamic Vendor Risk Profiling Required to Replace One-Time Assessments as Supply Chain Attack Frequency Accelerates
description: Traditional one-time vendor security assessments are inadequate for modern supply chain attack threats, requiring organizations to implement continuous dynamic vendor risk profiling that monitors package registries, maintainer account status, and dependency integrity in real-time to detect supply chain compromises before they propagate.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in GraphQL Persisted Query Files Alter Query Logic and Data Access Permissions
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in GraphQL persisted query definition files where hidden Unicode characters alter query field selections and access permission checks, causing GraphQL servers to expose different data fields and bypass authorization filters from what developers see when reviewing persisted query definitions.
label: AV-300

title: Shai-Hulud Attack Creates Exponentially Expanding Graph of Compromised Resources Where Each Secret Becomes a New Attack Node
description: GitGuardian documented that the Shai-Hulud supply chain worm created an exponentially expanding graph of compromised resources where each stolen secret became a new node enabling access to additional systems, with stolen npm tokens enabling package backdooring, stolen GitHub tokens granting Actions secrets access, and AI tool credentials enabling environment reconnaissance.
label: AV-400

title: GlassWorm Combines Cryptocurrency Wallet Draining with Account Hijacking for Self-Propagation Across Developer Ecosystems
description: The GlassWorm VS Code worm combined three attack objectives simultaneously: draining cryptocurrency wallets for immediate financial gain, hijacking developer accounts for further VS Code extension marketplace spread, and dropping a persistent RAT for long-term access, demonstrating the increasing multi-objective sophistication of supply chain attack campaigns.
label: AV-410

title: Attackers Squat Popular Framework Name Variants on npm Before Official Packages Are Published to Intercept Early Adopters
description: Security researchers documented supply chain name squatting attacks where attackers pre-register plausible npm package name variants for anticipated new frameworks and tools before official packages are published, intercepting early adopters who install the package before discovering the legitimate version has a different name.
label: AV-200

title: npm Provenance Attestation Gap Allows Malicious Unsigned Packages to Be Served Alongside Legitimate Signed Versions
description: Security researchers documented that npm optional provenance attestation system creates a gap where registries serve both signed and unsigned packages for the same package name, allowing attackers with stolen publish tokens to release malicious unsigned versions that are installed by consumers who do not enforce signature verification.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in OpenTelemetry Configuration Files Redirect Telemetry Data to Attacker-Controlled Collectors
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in OpenTelemetry configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter exporter endpoint URLs and sampling rate configurations, causing application telemetry data including traces containing sensitive business logic and user data to be redirected to attacker-controlled collector endpoints.
label: AV-300

title: Hardware Supply Chain Attacks Manipulate Chips and Firmware During Manufacturing to Insert Persistent Backdoors Before Delivery
description: Security researchers documented hardware supply chain attacks where chips and firmware are manipulated during manufacturing or distribution to insert persistent backdoors, with the resulting malicious hardware delivered through legitimate distribution channels to end users who have no way to detect the compromise through standard software security measures.
label: AV-400

title: Shai-Hulud Campaign Demonstrates Perpetually Evolving Attack Pattern Iterating Through Versions 1.0 2.0 and 3.0 in Single Year
description: The Shai-Hulud supply chain worm demonstrated a perpetually evolving attack pattern releasing versions 1.0, 2.0, and 3.0 within a single year, with each iteration incorporating lessons from defensive responses to previous versions and refining techniques to evade detection measures implemented after earlier campaigns.
label: AV-410

title: Nation-State Actors Create Fake Long-Term Open Source Contributor Identities to Eventually Gain Maintainer Access for Supply Chain Attack
description: Following the XZ Utils pattern, security researchers documented nation-state actors creating fake long-term contributor identities in popular open source projects, making genuine code contributions over months or years before requesting maintainer access that would enable supply chain attacks with trusted package publication capabilities.
label: AV-200

title: Modern Supply Chain Attacks Bypass Network Perimeter Defenses by Exploiting Trust Relationships in Software Distribution
description: Security researchers documented that modern supply chain attacks consistently bypass traditional network perimeter defenses by exploiting implicit trust relationships in software distribution channels, with malicious code entering organization networks through legitimate package manager downloads that pass through firewalls and intrusion detection systems without triggering alerts.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Crossplane Composite Resource Definitions Alter Cloud Infrastructure Composition Logic
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Crossplane Composite Resource Definition files where hidden Unicode characters alter composition logic and managed resource specifications, causing Crossplane control planes to provision cloud infrastructure with different configurations from what platform engineering teams see during XRD review.
label: AV-300

title: Modern Ransomware Groups Leverage Supply Chain Vulnerabilities to Compromise Multiple Organizations Simultaneously for Maximum Extortion Impact
description: Security researchers documented that modern ransomware operations evolved to leverage software supply chain vulnerabilities for simultaneous multi-organization compromise, using compromised shared vendor software or managed service provider infrastructure to deploy ransomware across multiple victim organizations in coordinated attacks that maximize extortion negotiating leverage.
label: AV-400

title: Coordinated 2025 Supply Chain Attack Timeline s1ngularity August Shai-Hulud September GhostAction September GlassWorm October
description: Security researchers documented a coordinated 2025 supply chain attack timeline where the s1ngularity Nx compromise in August led to the Shai-Hulud worm in September, followed by the GhostAction PyPI campaign also in September, and then the GlassWorm VS Code worm in October, demonstrating unprecedented coordination between supply chain attack campaigns.
label: AV-410

title: Tiny Transitive npm Package Compromise Cascades to Affect Thousands of Dependent Applications Through Deep Dependency Chains
description: Security researchers documented that even tiny npm packages with minimal functionality can cascade to affect thousands of dependent applications through transitive dependency chains, with the left-pad and event-stream incidents demonstrating how compromising a single small package can disrupt the entire JavaScript ecosystem.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in JWT Configuration Files Alter Signing Algorithm and Secret Key Specifications
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in JWT configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter signing algorithm specifications and secret key references, potentially causing applications to accept JWTs signed with weaker algorithms or different keys than intended while appearing to enforce strong authentication requirements in configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: Shai-Hulud Worm Initial Access via Developer Account Phishing Before Using Package Systems for Self-Propagation
description: The Shai-Hulud supply chain worm used developer account phishing as its initial access vector, compromising individual developer credentials before using the stolen credentials to access package publishing systems and inject the worm into npm packages for automated self-propagation across the broader developer ecosystem.
label: AV-509

title: GlassWorm Targets VS Code Fork Ecosystems via Open VSX Registry Affecting Developers Using VSCodium Gitpod and Theia
description: The GlassWorm worm specifically targeted the Open VSX Registry used by VS Code forks including VSCodium, Gitpod, and Eclipse Theia in addition to the official Microsoft Extension Marketplace, affecting developers across all major VS Code ecosystem variants who use open source alternatives to Microsoft proprietary extension distribution.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious Transitive Dependencies Installed Without Developer Awareness Through Deep Dependency Chain Inclusion
description: Security researchers documented that malicious packages are frequently installed as unintentional transitive dependencies where developers have no awareness of the package presence in their build, with package managers automatically resolving multi-level dependency chains that include attacker-controlled packages several levels removed from any directly specified dependency.
label: AV-200

title: Cloud Provider Misconfiguration Causes Unintentional Supply Chain Disruption Equivalent to Deliberate Attack Impact
description: Security researchers documented that cloud provider misconfigurations including DNS failures and CDN routing errors cause unintentional supply chain disruptions with impact equivalent to deliberate attack scenarios, demonstrating that supply chain fragility extends to operational failure modes in addition to malicious attack vectors.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in webpack Configuration Files Alter Bundle Output Paths and Plugin Execution Order
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in webpack configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter bundle output directory paths and plugin execution order, causing webpack builds to write output to attacker-accessible locations or execute malicious plugins with elevated build permissions invisible during webpack configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: Code Signing Certificate Theft Enables Malicious Software to Be Distributed with Legitimate Digital Signatures Bypassing OS Security
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks involving theft of code signing certificates from software vendors, enabling attackers to digitally sign malicious software with legitimate certificates that bypass operating system security warnings and antivirus scanning tools that trust signed executables from known publishers.
label: AV-509

title: AI Training Data Supply Chain Poisoning Introduces Backdoors into Machine Learning Models Affecting Downstream Applications
description: Security researchers documented AI training data supply chain poisoning attacks where malicious actors introduce carefully crafted poisoned examples into public training datasets, causing models trained on the compromised data to exhibit hidden backdoor behaviors that affect all downstream applications built on the poisoned foundation models.
label: AV-400
